_comment: Provenance manifest for the SAE golden vectors under tests/vectors/saevm/. The blocks/*, settlement/*, and recovery/recovery_transcript.json vectors are SELF-CONSISTENT, RUST-COMPUTED goldens that freeze the ava-saevm pipeline's own outputs (a FakeExecutor with deterministic, height-derived fake roots), committed to guard against silent regression — they are NOT, and by fixture design cannot be, byte-identical to a live Go saevm node (the Go node builds entirely different blocks with real txs + real firewood/geth roots). Exact Go/geth byte parity is instead asserted by the M7.29 differential, whose LIVE-Go-extracted corpus lives under recovery_differential/ (see that entry below): the Rust SAE recovery pipeline is driven over the real Go node's emitted block WIRE BYTES + committed ExecutionResults, and its reconstructed A/E/S frontier + LastSettled choice + block hashes are asserted equal to the Go node's. To re-freeze the Rust-computed goldens after an intentional change, run `cargo test -p ava-saevm-core --test golden -- --ignored --nocapture` (and, for blocks/block_hash.json, ava-saevm-blocks tests/block_hash_golden.rs) and paste the printed values; to re-freeze the live Go corpus see tests/differential/go-oracle/README.md.

go_reference.note: Reference inputs only — not a transliteration target (EVM = reth in avalanche-rs). Commit not pinned in this sandbox; M7.29 differential pins exact parity.

vectors.what: block_hash = keccak256(RLP(header)) of a single fixed hand-built reference header.
block hashes of every block in the fixed 9-element chain (genesis + 8 live-built blocks).
vectors.computed_by: the live VM builder + reth SealedBlock hashing in tests/golden.rs.

vectors.what: the settle() driver's (LastSettled height/hash, settled receipt root, settled state root) + A/E heights for the fixed chain.
vectors.computed_by: the live settle() driver (Tau=5s, unit-rate gas clock) in tests/golden.rs.

vectors.what: a crash+restart transcript: the reconstructed A/E/S heights + recovered executed/settled post-state roots after recover() rebuilds from disk at commit_interval=16.
vectors.computed_by: ava_saevm_core::recover() over a DiskState snapshot in tests/golden.rs (Rust-frozen, FakeExecutor fake roots — NOT Go-parity by fixture design; see recovery_differential/ for the live-Go-extracted recovery vectors).

vectors.what: LIVE Go-oracle crash+restart recovery vectors: per canonical height, the Go vms/saevm node's emitted block WIRE BYTES (RLP geth block) + committed ExecutionResults (gas-time, base fee, receipt/state roots), plus the Go source (pre-crash) and recovered (post-restart) A/E/S frontier observations, for three crash points. The Rust differential parse_block's the exact Go bytes (block-hash parity by construction), feeds them + the results into ava_saevm_core::recover(), and asserts the Rust-reconstructed A/E/S + LastSettled hash + roots == the Go node's.
vectors.computed_by: the live Go vms/saevm node via tests/differential/go-oracle/recovery_vector_emitter_test.go (avalanchego @ cc3b103b91173f5e8b89b1b31aea0816766c8ada, Go 1.25.10). Re-freeze: see tests/differential/go-oracle/README.md.

vectors.what: LIVE Go-oracle STREAMING per-barrier transcripts: an ordered barriers[] array (one record per accepted height = one AwaitFinalization barrier), each carrying the Go vms/saevm node's emitted block WIRE BYTES (RLP geth block) + committed ExecutionResults (FULL-PRECISION gas-time {seconds, frac_num, frac_denom}, base fee, receipt/state roots) + the A/E/S frontier observed AFTER that accept. Three scripted streams (commit interval 16 / archival=1 / 2s-blocks). The Rust differential drives its own Frontier + settle() walk block-by-block over the same stream and asserts the reconstructed S/E/A + settlement choice + LastSettled hash + roots + base fee == the Go node's at EVERY barrier index — validating the specs/00 §9 pipelined-commit optimization is observably neutral (the steady_settling commit-interval-16 and archival=1 streams reach an identical final A/E/S). Block hashes + the per-barrier settlement choice are genuinely RECOMPUTED by Rust (parse_block re-seal + last_to_settle_at walk over the emitted gas-times/build-times); roots + base fee round-trip (the emitter exposes only the resolved base_fee, not the per-block gas-clock excess/target an independent price() recompute would need — see the M7.32 follow-up).
vectors.computed_by: the live Go vms/saevm node via tests/differential/go-oracle/streaming_vector_emitter_test.go (avalanchego @ cc3b103b91173f5e8b89b1b31aea0816766c8ada, Go 1.25.10). Whole-second wall-clock advances keep the test-stub sub-second header component zero (matching the Go production cchain hook + the Rust whole-second Block::timestamp() model); the gas-time fraction is emitted + compared at full precision. Re-freeze: see tests/differential/go-oracle/README.md.
